segments.text: Then the LORD answered Job
out of the whirlwind, and said,
Who is this that darkeneth counsel by words without knowledge?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Gird up now thy loins like a man; for I will demand of thee, and answer thou me.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Where wast thou when I laid the foundations of the earth? declare, if thou hast understanding.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Who hath laid the measures thereof, if thou knowest? or who hath stretched the line upon it?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Whereupon are the foundations thereof fastened? or who laid the corner stone thereof;
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: When the morning stars sang together, and all the sons of God shouted for joy?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Or who shut up the sea with doors, when it brake forth, as if it had issued out of the womb?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: When I made the cloud the garment thereof, and thick darkness a swaddlingband for it,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And brake up for it my decreed place, and set bars and doors,
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: brake…: or, established my decree upon it

segments.text: And said, Hitherto shalt thou come, but no further: and here shall thy proud waves be stayed?
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: thy…: Heb. the pride of thy waves

segments.text: Hast thou commanded the morning since thy days; and caused the dayspring to know his place;
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: That it might take hold of the ends of the earth, that the wicked might be shaken out of it?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: It is turned as clay to the seal; and they stand as a garment.
And from the wicked their light is withholden, and the high arm shall be broken.
Hast thou entered into the springs of the sea? or hast thou walked in the search of the depth?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Have the gates of death been opened unto thee? or hast thou seen the doors of the shadow of death?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Hast thou perceived the breadth of the earth? declare if thou knowest it all.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Where is the way where light dwelleth? and as for darkness, where is the place thereof,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: That thou shouldest take it to the bound thereof, and that thou shouldest know the paths to the house thereof?
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: to the bound: or, at, etc

segments.text: Knowest thou it, because thou wast then born? or because the number of thy days is great?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Hast thou entered into the treasures of the snow? or hast thou seen the treasures of the hail,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Which I have reserved against the time of trouble, against the day of battle and war?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: By what way is the light parted, which scattereth the east wind upon the earth?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Who hath divided a watercourse for the overflowing of waters, or a way for the lightning of thunder;
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: To cause it to rain on the earth, where no man is; on the wilderness, wherein there is no man;
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: To satisfy the desolate and waste ground; and to cause the bud of the tender herb to spring forth?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Hath the rain a father? or who hath begotten the drops of dew?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Out of whose womb came the ice? and the hoary frost of heaven, who hath gendered it?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The waters are hid as with a stone, and the face of the deep is frozen.
Canst thou bind the sweet influences of Pleiades, or loose the bands of Orion?
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: Pleiades: or, the seven stars: Heb. Cimah

segments.text: Canst thou bring forth Mazzaroth in his season? or canst thou guide Arcturus with his sons?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Knowest thou the ordinances of heaven? canst thou set the dominion thereof in the earth?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Canst thou lift up thy voice to the clouds, that abundance of waters may cover thee?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Canst thou send lightnings, that they may go, and say unto thee, Here we are?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Who hath put wisdom in the inward parts? or who hath given understanding to the heart?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Who can number the clouds in wisdom? or who can stay the bottles of heaven,
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: stay: Heb. cause to lie down

segments.text: When the dust groweth into hardness, and the clods cleave fast together?
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: groweth…: or, is turned into mire: Heb. is poured

segments.text: Wilt thou hunt the prey for the lion? or fill the appetite of the young lions,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: When they couch in their dens, and abide in the covert to lie in wait?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Who provideth for the raven his food? when his young ones cry unto God, they wander for lack of meat.
segments.speaker: God
